export function buildExtractionSystemPrompt(input: { onboarding: boolean }): string {
  return [
    "Extract structured business entities and relationships from the provided text.",
    "Return only high-confidence extractions with explicit entity references.",
    "Entity kinds: project, feature, task, decision, question.",
    "Never emit kind: person.",
    "If a person is explicitly tied to an entity, set assignee_name on that entity using the literal source name.",
    "",
    "Output schema rules:",
    "- Each entity must include tempId, kind, text, confidence, evidence. For task, decision, and question entities also include category.",
    "- Entity text must be specific and concrete, not generic placeholders.",
    "- evidence must be a verbatim snippet from Current source text.",
    "- Always include tools and relationships arrays (can be empty).",
    "- confidence is a number between 0 and 1.",
    "",
    "Source grounding and context rules:",
    "- When Current message metadata exists, extract from Current source text only.",
    "- Conversation history is only for reference resolution (that/it/this/first option).",
    "- History must never add new entities that are absent from Current source text intent.",
    "- Do not copy assistant-only rationale/details from history into entity text.",
    "- Use the shortest canonical concept phrase when resolving references.",
    "- If entity meaning requires context resolution, include resolvedFromMessageId as the raw message id string only (UUID).",
    "- Never include wrappers or prefixes in resolvedFromMessageId (no [message:...], no message:...).",
    "- resolvedFromMessageId must be one of the allowed history message ids listed in the prompt section; otherwise omit it.",
    "- Prefer user messages as resolvedFromMessageId anchors when both user and assistant mention the same concept.",
    "- Never set resolvedFromMessageId to Current message id.",
    "- If Current message metadata is unavailable (document chunk extraction), do not emit resolvedFromMessageId.",
    "- If a confirmation cannot resolve to one specific concept, emit no entity.",
    "",
    "Kind classification rules:",
    "- Project: named product, system, or initiative that is a top-level workstream within the workspace (building, launching, creating, developing; identified by a proper noun or product name).",
    "- Decision: commitment/selection language (let's go with, move forward with, we decided, we'll use, I'm choosing, going with, settled on, committed to, approved).",
    "- Task: concrete executable work with an action verb (implement, build, fix, migrate, deploy, set up, write, test, review, handle, research, investigate, evaluate, analyze, explore).",
    "- Feature: capability/requirement or scoped artifact description (supports, provides, enables, needs to handle, must support, we need, requires; cards/dashboards/flows/screens/integrations).",
    "- Question: explicit question framing or question mark.",
    "- Decision vs feature test: did the user CHOOSE between alternatives, or DESCRIBE something to build? Choice language (X instead of Y, go with X, decided on X over Y) = one decision for the chosen option. Description language (we need X, build X) = feature. Even if the chosen option sounds like a feature, commitment language makes it a decision.",
    "- Prioritization/scoping statements about a specific capability without explicit execution action are feature (not project-level initiatives).",
    "- When the user explicitly labels something with a kind word ('the main feature is X', 'our project is called Y'), respect that classification unless workspace scope rules override it.",
    "- When a task describes work on a subject ('research pricing models'), extract only the task, not the subject as a separate project or feature.",
    "",
    "Category classification rules:",
    "- For task, decision, and question entities, classify category based on the area the entity affects.",
    "- Categories: engineering, research, marketing, operations, design, sales.",
    "- engineering: building, implementing, fixing, deploying code.",
    "- research: investigating, evaluating, comparing, exploring unknowns.",
    "- marketing: outreach, content, positioning, launch activities.",
    "- operations: setup, configuration, process, infrastructure admin.",
    "- design: wireframes, prototypes, UX, visual design, user flows.",
    "- sales: prospect outreach, demos, pitching, qualifying leads, choosing target segments, defining ICP, go-to-market targeting.",
    "- Classify by what the entity affects, not where it came up. 'Use JWT' is engineering even if discussed during design.",
    "- Set category to 'none' for project and feature entities.",
    "",
    "Project vs feature scope rule (overrides kind classification artifact hints):",
    "- Use the Workspace scope section to determine hierarchy level.",
    "- Entities at the top level of a workspace are projects. Capabilities within an existing project are features.",
    "- If the workspace itself is the product, its major workstreams are projects.",
    "- When workspace scope lists no existing projects, a new independent capability is a project regardless of artifact-type nouns (dashboard, flow, module, etc.). This rule only applies when workspace scope is present.",
    "- If projects already exist, new capabilities described within them are features.",
    "- A clearly independent domain or workstream (e.g. compliance reporting, analytics) alongside existing projects is a new project, not a feature of those projects.",
    "- A project would have features underneath it; a feature would have tasks underneath it.",
    "",
    "Question option suppression:",
    "- If one question includes options (X or Y), extract one question only.",
    "- Do not extract the options as separate decision/task entities.",
    "",
    "Tool and technology filtering:",
    "- Add tools only when adoption is explicit: we use, our stack includes, built with, stored in, integrated with, chose, running on, deployed on.",
    "- If a decision is resolved from context to a specific adopted tool (for example first option => SurrealDB), include that resolved tool in tools.",
    "- Reference-only mentions are not tools: existing tools like, competitors include, unlike, alternatives to, considered but rejected.",
    "- If text says tools are not what we are building, include none of those tools.",
    "- Tool-only adoption statements (our stack includes X) may produce tools with zero entities.",
    "- When adoption uses commitment language (we'll use X, we decided on X), also emit a decision entity alongside the tool.",
    "",
    "Placeholder rejection:",
    "- Do not extract vague references: my project, the project, our tool, the feature, the thing, this idea, that thing, my app, it, this, that.",
    "- Exception: resolve pronouns only when context maps to one specific named concept.",
    "- If the noun phrase is generic and no concrete named concept appears, extract nothing from that sentence.",
    "",
    "Conjunction extraction:",
    "- When a sentence lists multiple distinct items with 'and' or commas (e.g. 'OAuth and SSO', 'alerts and digests', 'CSV export and PDF generation'), extract each as a separate entity.",
    "- This applies to both features under a project and projects under a workspace.",
    "",
    "Document text behavior:",
    "- Document/spec text can contain entities without conversational phrasing.",
    "- Extract only the core committed entities; do not split one clause into multiple near-duplicate entities.",
    "- Prefer one dominant entity per clause unless there are clearly separate commitments.",
    "",
    "Examples:",
    "- Input: I decided to use event sourcing for audit logs. => decision text: use event sourcing (no person entity). If 'audit logs' exists in graph context, emit RELATES_TO from decision to that feature.",
    "- Input: Let's move forward with schema validation first. => one decision text: schema validation (not task + decision).",
    "- Input: We'll go with a single-page onboarding flow instead of multi-step. => one decision text: single-page onboarding flow (not two features; selection language means decision).",
    "- Input: Our stack includes SurrealDB and Bun. => entities: none; tools: SurrealDB, Bun.",
    "- Input: Existing PM tools like Linear and Notion are not what we are building. => entities: none; tools: none.",
    "- Input: We considered Redis but chose Memcached for caching. => decision text includes Memcached; tools include Memcached only.",
    "- Input: We'll use PostgreSQL for the main database. => decision text: use PostgreSQL; tools include PostgreSQL.",
    "- Input: Yes, let's go with that. Context has assistant recommendation with details and earlier user mention of SurrealDB. => decision text: SurrealDB; tools include SurrealDB; do not include assistant-only rationale phrases; set resolvedFromMessageId to user message id that introduced SurrealDB.",
    "- Input: Ok let's go with the first option for the graph. Context options are SurrealDB and Postgres. => decision text: SurrealDB; tools include SurrealDB; resolvedFromMessageId points to message with SurrealDB option.",
    "- Input: My project needs work. => entities: none; tools: none.",
    "- Input: The feature is broken. => entities: none; tools: none.",
    "- Input: I'm building Schack Systems and shipping the first release in April. => project: Schack Systems; decision: shipping the first release in April.",
    "- Input: The main feature is real-time conflict detection. => feature text: real-time conflict detection (user explicitly labels it as a feature).",
    "- Input: We need to research pricing models for B2B SaaS. => task text: research pricing models for B2B SaaS; do not extract 'pricing models' as a separate project.",
    "- Input: We need to build the payment processing and the user onboarding flows. Workspace: FinVault, no existing projects. => project: payment processing; project: user onboarding flows (two separate workstreams).",
    "- Input: We need OAuth and SSO support. Workspace: FinVault, existing project: User Management. => feature: OAuth support; feature: SSO support (capabilities within an existing project).",
    "- Input: We need CSV export and PDF generation for the reports. Workspace: TradeSync, existing project: Compliance Reporting. => feature: CSV export; feature: PDF generation (two separate features under existing project).",
    "- Input: The notification system needs to handle alerts and digests. Workspace: TradeSync, no existing projects. => project: notification system; feature: alerts; feature: digests (project plus two separate features).",
    "- Input: The platform will ingest chat, extract decisions and tasks, store them in SurrealDB, and render component cards for high-confidence entities. => task: extract decisions and tasks; decision: store them in SurrealDB; avoid extra entities for ingest/render clause fragments.",
    "- Input: Deduplicate features by embedding similarity and keep the more descriptive name when merged. => feature: Deduplicate features by embedding similarity (single entity).",
    "",
    "Relationships:",
    "- Include only relationships that are explicitly grounded in the Current source text.",
    "- Each relationship must include kind, fromTempId, toTempId, confidence, fromText, toText.",
    "- Use uppercase snake case relationship kinds when possible (for example DEPENDS_ON, BLOCKS, RELATES_TO).",
    "- When an extracted entity references an existing graph entity (e.g. a decision mentions an existing feature), emit a RELATES_TO relationship linking them.",
    input.onboarding
      ? "Prioritize foundational onboarding entities: projects, first decisions, open questions, and constraints."
      : "Prioritize actionable entities and direct relationships.",
  ].join("\n");
}

content.thinking: Now let me look at the cache file to see the actual extraction results for the failing cases, so I can understand what the model is extracting versus what's expected.

content.text: Let me check the cache to see what the model actually extracted for the failing cases.

input.command:
cat eval-results/cache/extraction-cache.json | python3 -c "
import json, sys
data = json.load(sys.stdin)
failing = ['long-context-first-option-resolution', 'decision-with-feature-link', 'project-no-keyword-launch', 'feature-within-project', 'document-like-chunk-1']
for key, val in data.items():
    case_id = val.get('caseId', '')
    if case_id in failing:
        print(f'=== {case_id} ===')
        print(f'  Input: {val.get(\"input\", \"\")[:120]}')
        print(f'  Extracted entities:')
        for e in val.get('extractedEntities', []):
            print(f'    - kind={e[\"kind\"]}, text={e[\"text\"]}, cat={e.get(\"category\",\"n/a\")}')
        print(f'  Extracted tools: {val.get(\"extractedTools\", [])}')
        print(f'  Extracted relations: {val.get(\"extractedRelations\", [])}')
        print(f'  Evidence rows:')
        for r in val.get('evidenceRows', []):
            print(f'    - resolvedFromId={r.get(\"resolvedFromId\")}, fromText={r.get(\"fromText\",\"\")[:80]}')
        print()
"
input.description: Inspect cache for failing eval cases

content.thinking:
Now I can clearly see the issues:

1. **long-context-first-option-resolution (72%)**: The model extracts `text="go with the first option for the graph"` but expected `text_contains="SurrealDB"`. The model fails to resolve "first option" to "SurrealDB". Precision and recall are both 0.00 because entity-precision checks if extracted text matches expected text, and "go with the first option for the graph" doesn't contain "SurrealDB".

2. **decision-with-feature-link (88%)**: The model extracts the decision correctly but fails to create a RELATES_TO relationship to the existing "audit logs" feature. 

3. **project-no-keyword-launch (92%)**: Only extracts 1 of 2 expected projects. Extracts "payment processing" but misses "onboarding" (expected: `project_contains "onboarding"`). Recall 0.50.

4. **feature-within-project (92%)**: Only extracts 1 of 2 expected features. Extracts "OAuth support" but misses "SSO" (expected: `feature_contains "SSO"`). Recall 0.50.

5. **document-like-chunk-1 (94%)**: Extracts 5 entities but only 2 expected. Precision is 0.40 (2/5). Over-extracting "The platform" as project, "ingest chat" and "render component cards" as tasks.

The most critical issue is the long-context resolution failure at 72% — the model isn't connecting "the first option" back to "SurrealDB" from earlier in the conversation. The prompt already has an example for this, so I need to strengthen how it handles anaphora. The other failures are mostly recall issues where the model misses secondary mentions, or precision problems where it's too liberal with entity classification. I should prioritize fixing the reference resolution since that's the biggest accuracy gap. I'm looking at a reference resolution issue where the model extracted the correct tool (SurrealDB) but failed to resolve the pronoun "first option" to its canonical name in the decision text. The prompt needs to be more explicit about requiring text resolution to the actual entity name rather than preserving the original phrasing.

For the second case, the model correctly identified the decision about event sourcing but didn't create the RELATES_TO relationship to the existing "audit logs" feature even though the prompt example explicitly covers this scenario. The relationship extraction logic seems to be missing despite clear guidance in the rules.

Looking at the second project issue, the model extracted only one project when the input clearly lists two distinct workstreams with "and" — payment processing and user onboarding flows. The conjunction rule is in the prompt, so this appears to be a failure in applying that splitting logic to this particular case.

For the feature-within-project case, the model got OAuth but missed SSO even though the golden example shows both should be extracted separately. The input phrasing is slightly different ("The authentication module needs to support OAuth and SSO" versus the example format), which might be throwing off the extraction.

The document-like-chunk case is over-extracting significantly — pulling out 5 entities instead of the expected 2. The model is treating "The platform" as a separate project and creating duplicate tasks around ingesting chat and rendering components, when the prompt explicitly says to avoid splitting one clause into multiple near-duplicate entities and to prefer one dominant entity per clause.

I need to bump the cache version since changing the prompt won't invalidate the existing cache entries — the cache key is built from the test case hash, not the prompt hash. Once I do that, I can focus on strengthening the prompt rules, particularly around reference resolution for phrases like "first option" and being more aggressive about consolidating related commitments into single entities rather than fragmenting them. The prompt already has a direct example for this case, but the model isn't following it—this could be a capability issue or the example needs more prominence. I'm going to strengthen the reference resolution rule by explicitly instructing the model to resolve referential phrases like "the first option" or "that option" to their concrete canonical names rather than keeping the vague text. For the decision-with-feature-link case, the model extracts the entity correctly but misses the RELATES_TO relationship even though the prompt covers it, so the instruction might need reinforcement or the model isn't picking up the graph context. The conjunction extraction is also failing despite an existing example, which suggests either a temperature or model consistency issue that needs the rule reinforced.

Looking at the over-extraction problem with "The platform" being incorrectly extracted as a project, the placeholder rejection rule is too loose—I need to tighten it to explicitly exclude vague references like "the platform" and "the project" that lack specificity.

content.text:
Now I can see exactly what's failing. Let me analyze each:

1. **long-context-first-option-resolution (72%)**: Model extracts `text="go with the first option for the graph"` instead of resolving to `"SurrealDB"`. The reference wasn't resolved in the entity text despite tools=[SurrealDB] being correct.

2. **decision-with-feature-link (88%)**: Decision extracted correctly but RELATES_TO relationship to seeded "audit logs" feature is missing.

3. **project-no-keyword-launch (92%)**: Only extracted "payment processing", missed "onboarding" (conjunction failure).

4. **feature-within-project (92%)**: Only extracted "OAuth support", missed "SSO" (conjunction failure).

5. **document-like-chunk-1 (94%)**: Over-extracted 5 entities (3 extra tasks + "The platform" as project) instead of expected 2.

Let me check the scorers to understand the scoring mechanics better before making changes.

content.thinking:
Now I understand the scoring completely. For `long-context-first-option-resolution`:
- Expected: `{ kind: "decision", text_contains: "SurrealDB" }`
- Extracted: `{ kind: "decision", text: "go with the first option for the graph" }`
- Match check: `normalizeForSubstring("go with the first option for the graph").includes(normalizeForSubstring("SurrealDB"))` → `"go with the first option for the graph".includes("surrealdb")` → false
- So precision = 0/1 = 0, recall = 0/1 = 0


This is a critical failure—the model completely missed the reference resolution. Looking at `decision-with-feature-link`, the expected entity is `{ kind: "decision", text_contains: "event sourcing" }` and the extracted text "use event sourcing for audit logs" actually contains that substring, so it scores perfectly on precision and recall. The 88% overall score must be dragged down by missing relation-recall metrics.

I'm noticing the Avg column shows 0.96 while the Score column shows 88%—these are calculated differently. The Score appears to be evalite's built-in scoring mechanism rather than my weighted average, so I need to understand how evalite computes that final score.

Looking at the column definitions...

Defining the columns structure... The relation-recall scorer is returning 0 because the extracted relations are empty, even though the expected relation should be there linking the decision to the feature with "audit logs" as the text. This zero score is pulling down both the weighted average and the overall Score calculation, which explains why the Avg shows 0.96 while Score is at 88%.

Looking at the full weighting breakdown, relation-recall only carries 0.03 weight in the multi_allowed configuration, so it's not the primary culprit. The real issue is that the Score column appears to be evalite's default calculation—likely a simple average across all 13 scorers—which means even one or two zeros significantly drag down the overall percentage.

I should focus on the prompt changes that will address the most impactful failures rather than getting caught up in the exact scoring mechanics.

The first-option-resolution failure at 72% is critical—the model isn't resolving referential phrases like "the first option" to their actual concept names even though the prompt already contains an example for this. I need to add an explicit, stronger rule that forces resolution of any referential language to the specific named concept from context, ensuring the entity text never contains the referential phrase itself.

For the conjunction extraction failures in project-no-keyword-launch and feature-within-project, both at 92%, the prompt already has the rule and examples, but the model is only pulling the first item. I can tighten the conjunction rule to be more forceful about extracting all connected items.

The document-like-chunk-1 failure at 94% is lower priority, but I should still address it once I've handled the higher-impact issues.

For decision-with-feature-link at 88%, the missing RELATES_TO relationship suggests a model compliance issue since the prompt and examples already cover this case.

I'm going to revise the prompt to strengthen reference resolution, add common placeholder terms like "the platform," "the system," and "the app" to the rejection list, tighten the conjunction extraction rule, and reinforce the relationship emission rule. I'll also bump the cache version to force re-extraction.

export function buildExtractionSystemPrompt(input: { onboarding: boolean }): string {
  return [
    "Extract structured business entities and relationships from the provided text.",
    "Return only high-confidence extractions with explicit entity references.",
    "Entity kinds: project, feature, task, decision, question.",
    "Never emit kind: person.",
    "If a person is explicitly tied to an entity, set assignee_name on that entity using the literal source name.",
    "",
    "Output schema rules:",
    "- Each entity must include tempId, kind, text, confidence, evidence. For task, decision, and question entities also include category.",
    "- Entity text must be specific and concrete, not generic placeholders.",
    "- evidence must be a verbatim snippet from Current source text.",
    "- Always include tools and relationships arrays (can be empty).",
    "- confidence is a number between 0 and 1.",
    "",
    "Source grounding and context rules:",
    "- When Current message metadata exists, extract from Current source text only.",
    "- Conversation history is only for reference resolution (that/it/this/first option).",
    "- History must never add new entities that are absent from Current source text intent.",
    "- Do not copy assistant-only rationale/details from history into entity text.",
    "- Use the shortest canonical concept phrase when resolving references.",
    "- If entity meaning requires context resolution, include resolvedFromMessageId as the raw message id string only (UUID).",
    "- Never include wrappers or prefixes in resolvedFromMessageId (no [message:...], no message:...).",
    "- resolvedFromMessageId must be one of the allowed history message ids listed in the prompt section; otherwise omit it.",
    "- Prefer user messages as resolvedFromMessageId anchors when both user and assistant mention the same concept.",
    "- Never set resolvedFromMessageId to Current message id.",
    "- If Current message metadata is unavailable (document chunk extraction), do not emit resolvedFromMessageId.",
    "- If a confirmation cannot resolve to one specific concept, emit no entity.",
    "",
    "Kind classification rules:",
    "- Project: named product, system, or initiative that is a top-level workstream within the workspace (building, launching, creating, developing; identified by a proper noun or product name).",
    "- Decision: commitment/selection language (let's go with, move forward with, we decided, we'll use, I'm choosing, going with, settled on, committed to, approved).",
    "- Task: concrete executable work with an action verb (implement, build, fix, migrate, deploy, set up, write, test, review, handle, research, investigate, evaluate, analyze, explore).",
    "- Feature: capability/requirement or scoped artifact description (supports, provides, enables, needs to handle, must support, we need, requires; cards/dashboards/flows/screens/integrations).",
    "- Question: explicit question framing or question mark.",
    "- Decision vs feature test: did the user CHOOSE between alternatives, or DESCRIBE something to build? Choice language (X instead of Y, go with X, decided on X over Y) = one decision for the chosen option. Description language (we need X, build X) = feature. Even if the chosen option sounds like a feature, commitment language makes it a decision.",
    "- Prioritization/scoping statements about a specific capability without explicit execution action are feature (not project-level initiatives).",
    "- When the user explicitly labels something with a kind word ('the main feature is X', 'our project is called Y'), respect that classification unless workspace scope rules override it.",
    "- When a task describes work on a subject ('research pricing models'), extract only the task, not the subject as a separate project or feature.",
    "",
    "Category classification rules:",
    "- For task, decision, and question entities, classify category based on the area the entity affects.",
    "- Categories: engineering, research, marketing, operations, design, sales.",
    "- engineering: building, implementing, fixing, deploying code.",
    "- research: investigating, evaluating, comparing, exploring unknowns.",
    "- marketing: outreach, content, positioning, launch activities.",
    "- operations: setup, configuration, process, infrastructure admin.",
    "- design: wireframes, prototypes, UX, visual design, user flows.",
    "- sales: prospect outreach, demos, pitching, qualifying leads, choosing target segments, defining ICP, go-to-market targeting.",
    "- Classify by what the entity affects, not where it came up. 'Use JWT' is engineering even if discussed during design.",
    "- Set category to 'none' for project and feature entities.",
    "",
    "Project vs feature scope rule (overrides kind classification artifact hints):",
    "- Use the Workspace scope section to determine hierarchy level.",
    "- Entities at the top level of a workspace are projects. Capabilities within an existing project are features.",
    "- If the workspace itself is the product, its major workstreams are projects.",
    "- When workspace scope lists no existing projects, a new independent capability is a project regardless of artifact-type nouns (dashboard, flow, module, etc.). This rule only applies when workspace scope is present.",
    "- If projects already exist, new capabilities described within them are features.",
    "- A clearly independent domain or workstream (e.g. compliance reporting, analytics) alongside existing projects is a new project, not a feature of those projects.",
    "- A project would have features underneath it; a feature would have tasks underneath it.",
    "",
    "Question option suppression:",
    "- If one question includes options (X or Y), extract one question only.",
    "- Do not extract the options as separate decision/task entities.",
    "",
    "Tool and technology filtering:",
    "- Add tools only when adoption is explicit: we use, our stack includes, built with, stored in, integrated with, chose, running on, deployed on.",
    "- If a decision is resolved from context to a specific adopted tool (for example first option => SurrealDB), include that resolved tool in tools.",
    "- Reference-only mentions are not tools: existing tools like, competitors include, unlike, alternatives to, considered but rejected.",
    "- If text says tools are not what we are building, include none of those tools.",
    "- Tool-only adoption statements (our stack includes X) may produce tools with zero entities.",
    "- When adoption uses commitment language (we'll use X, we decided on X), also emit a decision entity alongside the tool.",
    "",
    "Placeholder rejection:",
    "- Do not extract vague references: my project, the project, our tool, the feature, the thing, this idea, that thing, my app, it, this, that.",
    "- Exception: resolve pronouns only when context maps to one specific named concept.",
    "- If the noun phrase is generic and no concrete named concept appears, extract nothing from that sentence.",
    "",
    "Conjunction extraction:",
    "- When a sentence lists multiple distinct items with 'and' or commas (e.g. 'OAuth and SSO', 'alerts and digests', 'CSV export and PDF generation'), extract each as a separate entity.",
    "- This applies to both features under a project and projects under a workspace.",
    "",
    "Document text behavior:",
    "- Document/spec text can contain entities without conversational phrasing.",
    "- Extract only the core committed entities; do not split one clause into multiple near-duplicate entities.",
    "- Prefer one dominant entity per clause unless there are clearly separate commitments.",
    "",
    "Examples:",
    "- Input: I decided to use event sourcing for audit logs. => decision text: use event sourcing (no person entity). If 'audit logs' exists in graph context, emit RELATES_TO from decision to that feature.",
    "- Input: Let's move forward with schema validation first. => one decision text: schema validation (not task + decision).",
    "- Input: We'll go with a single-page onboarding flow instead of multi-step. => one decision text: single-page onboarding flow (not two features; selection language means decision).",
    "- Input: Our stack includes SurrealDB and Bun. => entities: none; tools: SurrealDB, Bun.",
    "- Input: Existing PM tools like Linear and Notion are not what we are building. => entities: none; tools: none.",
    "- Input: We considered Redis but chose Memcached for caching. => decision text includes Memcached; tools include Memcached only.",
    "- Input: We'll use PostgreSQL for the main database. => decision text: use PostgreSQL; tools include PostgreSQL.",
    "- Input: Yes, let's go with that. Context has assistant recommendation with details and earlier user mention of SurrealDB. => decision text: SurrealDB; tools include SurrealDB; do not include assistant-only rationale phrases; set resolvedFromMessageId to user message id that introduced SurrealDB.",
    "- Input: Ok let's go with the first option for the graph. Context options are SurrealDB and Postgres. => decision text: SurrealDB; tools include SurrealDB; resolvedFromMessageId points to message with SurrealDB option.",
    "- Input: My project needs work. => entities: none; tools: none.",
    "- Input: The feature is broken. => entities: none; tools: none.",
    "- Input: I'm building Schack Systems and shipping the first release in April. => project: Schack Systems; decision: shipping the first release in April.",
    "- Input: The main feature is real-time conflict detection. => feature text: real-time conflict detection (user explicitly labels it as a feature).",
    "- Input: We need to research pricing models for B2B SaaS. => task text: research pricing models for B2B SaaS; do not extract 'pricing models' as a separate project.",
    "- Input: We need to build the payment processing and the user onboarding flows. Workspace: FinVault, no existing projects. => project: payment processing; project: user onboarding flows (two separate workstreams).",
    "- Input: We need OAuth and SSO support. Workspace: FinVault, existing project: User Management. => feature: OAuth support; feature: SSO support (capabilities within an existing project).",
    "- Input: We need CSV export and PDF generation for the reports. Workspace: TradeSync, existing project: Compliance Reporting. => feature: CSV export; feature: PDF generation (two separate features under existing project).",
    "- Input: The notification system needs to handle alerts and digests. Workspace: TradeSync, no existing projects. => project: notification system; feature: alerts; feature: digests (project plus two separate features).",
    "- Input: The platform will ingest chat, extract decisions and tasks, store them in SurrealDB, and render component cards for high-confidence entities. => task: extract decisions and tasks; decision: store them in SurrealDB; avoid extra entities for ingest/render clause fragments.",
    "- Input: Deduplicate features by embedding similarity and keep the more descriptive name when merged. => feature: Deduplicate features by embedding similarity (single entity).",
    "",
    "Relationships:",
    "- Include only relationships that are explicitly grounded in the Current source text.",
    "- Each relationship must include kind, fromTempId, toTempId, confidence, fromText, toText.",
    "- Use uppercase snake case relationship kinds when possible (for example DEPENDS_ON, BLOCKS, RELATES_TO).",
    "- When an extracted entity references an existing graph entity (e.g. a decision mentions an existing feature), emit a RELATES_TO relationship linking them.",
    input.onboarding
      ? "Prioritize foundational onboarding entities: projects, first decisions, open questions, and constraints."
      : "Prioritize actionable entities and direct relationships.",
  ].join("\n");
}

    "- research: investigating, evaluating, comparing, exploring unknowns.",
    "- marketing: outreach, content, positioning, launch activities.",
    "- operations: setup, configuration, process, infrastructure admin.",
    "- design: wireframes, prototypes, UX, visual design, user flows.",
    "- sales: prospect outreach, demos, pitching, qualifying leads, choosing target segments, defining ICP, go-to-market targeting.",
    "- Classify by what the entity affects, not where it came up. 'Use JWT' is engineering even if discussed during design.",
    "- Set category to 'none' for project and feature entities.",
    "",
    "Project vs feature scope rule (overrides kind classification artifact hints):",
    "- Use the Workspace scope section to determine hierarchy level.",
    "- Entities at the top level of a workspace are projects. Capabilities within an existing project are features.",
    "- If the workspace itself is the product, its major workstreams are projects.",
    "- When workspace scope lists no existing projects, a new independent capability is a project regardless of artifact-type nouns (dashboard, flow, module, etc.). This rule only applies when workspace scope is present.",
    "- If projects already exist, new capabilities described within them are features.",
    "- A clearly independent domain or workstream (e.g. compliance reporting, analytics) alongside existing projects is a new project, not a feature of those projects.",
    "- A project would have features underneath it; a feature would have tasks underneath it.",
    "",
    "Question option suppression:",
    "- If one question includes options (X or Y), extract one question only.",
    "- Do not extract the options as separate decision/task entities.",
    "",
    "Tool and technology filtering:",
    "- Add tools only when adoption is explicit: we use, our stack includes, built with, stored in, integrated with, chose, running on, deployed on.",
    "- If a decision is resolved from context to a specific adopted tool (for example first option => SurrealDB), include that resolved tool in tools.",
    "- Reference-only mentions are not tools: existing tools like, competitors include, unlike, alternatives to, considered but rejected.",
    "- If text says tools are not what we are building, include none of those tools.",
    "- Tool-only adoption statements (our stack includes X) may produce tools with zero entities.",
    "- When adoption uses commitment language (we'll use X, we decided on X), also emit a decision entity alongside the tool.",
    "",
    "Placeholder rejection:",
    "- Do not extract vague references: my project, the project, our tool, the feature, the thing, this idea, that thing, my app, it, this, that.",
    "- Exception: resolve pronouns only when context maps to one specific named concept.",
    "- If the noun phrase is generic and no concrete named concept appears, extract nothing from that sentence.",
    "",
    "Conjunction extraction:",
    "- When a sentence lists multiple distinct items with 'and' or commas (e.g. 'OAuth and SSO', 'alerts and digests', 'CSV export and PDF generation'), extract each as a separate entity.",
    "- This applies to both features under a project and projects under a workspace.",
    "",
    "Document text behavior:",
    "- Document/spec text can contain entities without conversational phrasing.",
    "- Extract only the core committed entities; do not split one clause into multiple near-duplicate entities.",

    "- Input: We considered Redis but chose Memcached for caching. => decision text includes Memcached; tools include Memcached only.",
    "- Input: We'll use PostgreSQL for the main database. => decision text: use PostgreSQL; tools include PostgreSQL.",
    "- Input: Yes, let's go with that. Context has assistant recommendation with details and earlier user mention of SurrealDB. => decision text: SurrealDB; tools include SurrealDB; do not include assistant-only rationale phrases; set resolvedFromMessageId to user message id that introduced SurrealDB.",
    "- Input: Ok let's go with the first option for the graph. Context options are SurrealDB and Postgres. => decision text: SurrealDB; tools include SurrealDB; resolvedFromMessageId points to message with SurrealDB option.",
    "- Input: My project needs work. => entities: none; tools: none.",
    "- Input: The feature is broken. => entities: none; tools: none.",
    "- Input: I'm building Schack Systems and shipping the first release in April. => project: Schack Systems; decision: shipping the first release in April.",
    "- Input: The main feature is real-time conflict detection. => feature text: real-time conflict detection (user explicitly labels it as a feature).",
    "- Input: We need to research pricing models for B2B SaaS. => task text: research pricing models for B2B SaaS; do not extract 'pricing models' as a separate project.",
    "- Input: We need to build the payment processing and the user onboarding flows. Workspace: FinVault, no existing projects. => project: payment processing; project: user onboarding flows (two separate workstreams).",
    "- Input: We need OAuth and SSO support. Workspace: FinVault, existing project: User Management. => feature: OAuth support; feature: SSO support (capabilities within an existing project).",
    "- Input: We need CSV export and PDF generation for the reports. Workspace: TradeSync, existing project: Compliance Reporting. => feature: CSV export; feature: PDF generation (two separate features under existing project).",
    "- Input: The notification system needs to handle alerts and digests. Workspace: TradeSync, no existing projects. => project: notification system; feature: alerts; feature: digests (project plus two separate features).",
    "- Input: The platform will ingest chat, extract decisions and tasks, store them in SurrealDB, and render component cards for high-confidence entities. => task: extract decisions and tasks; decision: store them in SurrealDB; avoid extra entities for ingest/render clause fragments.",
    "- Input: Deduplicate features by embedding similarity and keep the more descriptive name when merged. => feature: Deduplicate features by embedding similarity (single entity).",
    "",
    "Relationships:",
    "- Include only relationships that are explicitly grounded in the Current source text.",
    "- Each relationship must include kind, fromTempId, toTempId, confidence, fromText, toText.",
    "- Use uppercase snake case relationship kinds when possible (for example DEPENDS_ON, BLOCKS, RELATES_TO).",
    "- When an extracted entity references an existing graph entity (e.g. a decision mentions an existing feature), emit a RELATES_TO relationship linking them.",
    input.onboarding
      ? "Prioritize foundational onboarding entities: projects, first decisions, open questions, and constraints."
      : "Prioritize actionable entities and direct relationships.",
  ].join("\n");
}

export function buildExtractionSystemPrompt(input: { onboarding: boolean }): string {
  return [
    "Extract structured business entities and relationships from the provided text.",
    "Return only high-confidence extractions with explicit entity references.",
    "Entity kinds: project, feature, task, decision, question.",
    "Never emit kind: person.",
    "If a person is explicitly tied to an entity, set assignee_name on that entity using the literal source name.",
    "",
    "Output schema rules:",
    "- Each entity must include tempId, kind, text, confidence, evidence. For task, decision, and question entities also include category.",
    "- Entity text must be specific and concrete, not generic placeholders.",
    "- evidence must be a verbatim snippet from Current source text.",
    "- Always include tools and relationships arrays (can be empty).",
    "- confidence is a number between 0 and 1.",
    "",
    "Source grounding and context rules:",
    "- When Current message metadata exists, extract from Current source text only.",
    "- Conversation history is only for reference resolution (that/it/this/first option).",
    "- History must never add new entities that are absent from Current source text intent.",
    "- Do not copy assistant-only rationale/details from history into entity text.",
    "- Use the shortest canonical concept phrase when resolving references. Entity text must always contain the resolved concept name (e.g. 'SurrealDB'), never the referential phrase (e.g. 'the first option').",
    "- If entity meaning requires context resolution, include resolvedFromMessageId as the raw message id string only (UUID).",
    "- Never include wrappers or prefixes in resolvedFromMessageId (no [message:...], no message:...).",
    "- resolvedFromMessageId must be one of the allowed history message ids listed in the prompt section; otherwise omit it.",
    "- Prefer user messages as resolvedFromMessageId anchors when both user and assistant mention the same concept.",
    "- Never set resolvedFromMessageId to Current message id.",
    "- If Current message metadata is unavailable (document chunk extraction), do not emit resolvedFromMessageId.",
    "- If a confirmation cannot resolve to one specific concept, emit no entity.",
    "",
    "Kind classification rules:",
    "- Project: named product, system, or initiative that is a top-level workstream within the workspace (building, launching, creating, developing; identified by a proper noun or product name).",
    "- Decision: commitment/selection language (let's go with, move forward with, we decided, we'll use, I'm choosing, going with, settled on, committed to, approved).",
    "- Task: concrete executable work with an action verb (implement, build, fix, migrate, deploy, set up, write, test, review, handle, research, investigate, evaluate, analyze, explore).",
    "- Feature: capability/requirement or scoped artifact description (supports, provides, enables, needs to handle, must support, we need, requires; cards/dashboards/flows/screens/integrations).",
    "- Question: explicit question framing or question mark.",
    "- Decision vs feature test: did the user CHOOSE between alternatives, or DESCRIBE something to build? Choice language (X instead of Y, go with X, decided on X over Y) = one decision for the chosen option. Description language (we need X, build X) = feature. Even if the chosen option sounds like a feature, commitment language makes it a decision.",
    "- Prioritization/scoping statements about a specific capability without explicit execution action are feature (not project-level initiatives).",
    "- When the user explicitly labels something with a kind word ('the main feature is X', 'our project is called Y'), respect that classification unless workspace scope rules override it.",
    "- When a task describes work on a subject ('research pricing models'), extract only the task, not the subject as a separate project or feature.",
    "",
    "Category classification rules:",
    "- For task, decision, and question entities, classify category based on the area the entity affects.",
    "- Categories: engineering, research, marketing, operations, design, sales.",
    "- engineering: building, implementing, fixing, deploying code.",
    "- research: investigating, evaluating, comparing, exploring unknowns.",
    "- marketing: outreach, content, positioning, launch activities.",
    "- operations: setup, configuration, process, infrastructure admin.",
    "- design: wireframes, prototypes, UX, visual design, user flows.",
    "- sales: prospect outreach, demos, pitching, qualifying leads, choosing target segments, defining ICP, go-to-market targeting.",
    "- Classify by what the entity affects, not where it came up. 'Use JWT' is engineering even if discussed during design.",
    "- Set category to 'none' for project and feature entities.",
    "",
    "Project vs feature scope rule (overrides kind classification artifact hints):",
    "- Use the Workspace scope section to determine hierarchy level.",
    "- Entities at the top level of a workspace are projects. Capabilities within an existing project are features.",
    "- If the workspace itself is the product, its major workstreams are projects.",
    "- When workspace scope lists no existing projects, a new independent capability is a project regardless of artifact-type nouns (dashboard, flow, module, etc.). This rule only applies when workspace scope is present.",
    "- If projects already exist, new capabilities described within them are features.",
    "- A clearly independent domain or workstream (e.g. compliance reporting, analytics) alongside existing projects is a new project, not a feature of those projects.",
    "- A project would have features underneath it; a feature would have tasks underneath it.",
    "",
    "Question option suppression:",
    "- If one question includes options (X or Y), extract one question only.",
    "- Do not extract the options as separate decision/task entities.",
    "",
    "Tool and technology filtering:",
    "- Add tools only when adoption is explicit: we use, our stack includes, built with, stored in, integrated with, chose, running on, deployed on.",
    "- If a decision is resolved from context to a specific adopted tool (for example first option => SurrealDB), include that resolved tool in tools.",
    "- Reference-only mentions are not tools: existing tools like, competitors include, unlike, alternatives to, considered but rejected.",
    "- If text says tools are not what we are building, include none of those tools.",
    "- Tool-only adoption statements (our stack includes X) may produce tools with zero entities.",
    "- When adoption uses commitment language (we'll use X, we decided on X), also emit a decision entity alongside the tool.",
    "",
    "Placeholder rejection:",
    "- Do not extract vague references: my project, the project, our tool, the feature, the thing, this idea, that thing, my app, the platform, the system, it, this, that.",
    "- Exception: resolve pronouns only when context maps to one specific named concept.",
    "- If the noun phrase is generic and no concrete named concept appears, extract nothing from that sentence.",
    "",
    "Conjunction extraction:",
    "- When a sentence lists multiple distinct items with 'and' or commas (e.g. 'OAuth and SSO', 'alerts and digests', 'CSV export and PDF generation', 'payment processing and onboarding'), extract EACH item as a separate entity. Do not stop after the first item.",
    "- This applies to both features under a project and projects under a workspace.",
    "",
    "Document text behavior:",
    "- Document/spec text can contain entities without conversational phrasing.",
    "- Extract only the core committed entities; do not split one clause into multiple near-duplicate entities.",
    "- Prefer one dominant entity per clause unless there are clearly separate commitments.",
    "",
    "Examples:",
    "- Input: I decided to use event sourcing for audit logs. => decision text: use event sourcing (no person entity). If 'audit logs' exists in graph context, emit RELATES_TO from decision to that feature.",
    "- Input: Let's move forward with schema validation first. => one decision text: schema validation (not task + decision).",
    "- Input: We'll go with a single-page onboarding flow instead of multi-step. => one decision text: single-page onboarding flow (not two features; selection language means decision).",
    "- Input: Our stack includes SurrealDB and Bun. => entities: none; tools: SurrealDB, Bun.",
    "- Input: Existing PM tools like Linear and Notion are not what we are building. => entities: none; tools: none.",
    "- Input: We considered Redis but chose Memcached for caching. => decision text includes Memcached; tools include Memcached only.",
    "- Input: We'll use PostgreSQL for the main database. => decision text: use PostgreSQL; tools include PostgreSQL.",
    "- Input: Yes, let's go with that. Context has assistant recommendation with details and earlier user mention of SurrealDB. => decision text: SurrealDB; tools include SurrealDB; do not include assistant-only rationale phrases; set resolvedFromMessageId to user message id that introduced SurrealDB.",
    "- Input: Ok let's go with the first option for the graph. Context options are SurrealDB and Postgres. => decision text: SurrealDB; tools include SurrealDB; resolvedFromMessageId points to message with SurrealDB option.",
    "- Input: My project needs work. => entities: none; tools: none.",
    "- Input: The feature is broken. => entities: none; tools: none.",
    "- Input: I'm building Schack Systems and shipping the first release in April. => project: Schack Systems; decision: shipping the first release in April.",
    "- Input: The main feature is real-time conflict detection. => feature text: real-time conflict detection (user explicitly labels it as a feature).",
    "- Input: We need to research pricing models for B2B SaaS. => task text: research pricing models for B2B SaaS; do not extract 'pricing models' as a separate project.",
    "- Input: We need to build the payment processing and the user onboarding flows. Workspace: FinVault, no existing projects. => project: payment processing; project: user onboarding flows (two separate workstreams).",
    "- Input: We need OAuth and SSO support. Workspace: FinVault, existing project: User Management. => feature: OAuth support; feature: SSO support (capabilities within an existing project).",
    "- Input: We need CSV export and PDF generation for the reports. Workspace: TradeSync, existing project: Compliance Reporting. => feature: CSV export; feature: PDF generation (two separate features under existing project).",
    "- Input: The notification system needs to handle alerts and digests. Workspace: TradeSync, no existing projects. => project: notification system; feature: alerts; feature: digests (project plus two separate features).",
    "- Input: The platform will ingest chat, extract decisions and tasks, store them in SurrealDB, and render component cards for high-confidence entities. => task: extract decisions and tasks; decision: store them in SurrealDB; avoid extra entities for ingest/render clause fragments.",
    "- Input: Deduplicate features by embedding similarity and keep the more descriptive name when merged. => feature: Deduplicate features by embedding similarity (single entity).",
    "",
    "Relationships:",
    "- Include only relationships that are explicitly grounded in the Current source text.",
    "- Each relationship must include kind, fromTempId, toTempId, confidence, fromText, toText.",
    "- Use uppercase snake case relationship kinds when possible (for example DEPENDS_ON, BLOCKS, RELATES_TO).",
    "- When an extracted entity references an existing graph entity (e.g. a decision mentions an existing feature by name), ALWAYS emit a RELATES_TO relationship linking the new entity to the existing one. This is critical for graph connectivity.",
    input.onboarding
      ? "Prioritize foundational onboarding entities: projects, first decisions, open questions, and constraints."
      : "Prioritize actionable entities and direct relationships.",
  ].join("\n");
}

export function buildExtractionSystemPrompt(input: { onboarding: boolean }): string {
  return [
    "Extract structured business entities and relationships from the provided text.",
    "Return only high-confidence extractions with explicit entity references.",
    "Entity kinds: project, feature, task, decision, question.",
    "Never emit kind: person.",

content.text:
All 125 unit tests pass. Here's a summary of the changes I made:

**Prompt changes (`app/src/server/extraction/prompt.ts`):**

1. **Reference resolution** (fixes `long-context-first-option-resolution` at 72%): Strengthened the rule to explicitly say entity text must contain the resolved concept name (e.g. 'SurrealDB'), never the referential phrase (e.g. 'the first option').

2. **Placeholder rejection** (fixes `document-like-chunk-1` over-extraction): Added "the platform" and "the system" to the list of vague references that should not be extracted as entities.

3. **Conjunction extraction** (fixes `project-no-keyword-launch` and `feature-within-project` at 92%): Added "payment processing and onboarding" as an explicit example and emphasized "Do not stop after the first item."

4. **Relationship emission** (fixes `decision-with-feature-link` at 88%): Strengthened the RELATES_TO rule to say "ALWAYS emit" and "This is critical for graph connectivity."

5. **Document text over-extraction** (fixes `document-like-chunk-1` at 94%): Added rule that descriptive verbs in a system overview are not separate tasks.

**Cache bump (`evals/extraction.eval.ts`):** v11 → v12 to force re-extraction with the new prompt.

Please run `bun run eval` to verify the fixes work. The main target is getting `long-context-first-option-resolution` above 95% (it's at 72% - the worst case) and the other 4 cases above 95%.

diff --git a/app/src/server/extraction/prompt.ts b/app/src/server/extraction/prompt.ts
index 413d7a2..cc2a1c8 100644
--- a/app/src/server/extraction/prompt.ts
+++ b/app/src/server/extraction/prompt.ts
@@ -18,7 +18,7 @@ export function buildExtractionSystemPrompt(input: { onboarding: boolean }): str
     "- Conversation history is only for reference resolution (that/it/this/first option).",
     "- History must never add new entities that are absent from Current source text intent.",
     "- Do not copy assistant-only rationale/details from history into entity text.",
-    "- Use the shortest canonical concept phrase when resolving references.",
+    "- Use the shortest canonical concept phrase when resolving references. Entity text must always contain the resolved concept name (e.g. 'SurrealDB'), never the referential phrase (e.g. 'the first option').",
     "- If entity meaning requires context resolution, include resolvedFromMessageId as the raw message id string only (UUID).",
     "- Never include wrappers or prefixes in resolvedFromMessageId (no [message:...], no message:...).",
     "- resolvedFromMessageId must be one of the allowed history message ids listed in the prompt section; otherwise omit it.",
@@ -72,18 +72,19 @@ export function buildExtractionSystemPrompt(input: { onboarding: boolean }): str
     "- When adoption uses commitment language (we'll use X, we decided on X), also emit a decision entity alongside the tool.",
     "",
     "Placeholder rejection:",
-    "- Do not extract vague references: my project, the project, our tool, the feature, the thing, this idea, that thing, my app, it, this, that.",
+    "- Do not extract vague references: my project, the project, our tool, the feature, the thing, this idea, that thing, my app, the platform, the system, it, this, that.",
     "- Exception: resolve pronouns only when context maps to one specific named concept.",
     "- If the noun phrase is generic and no concrete named concept appears, extract nothing from that sentence.",
     "",
     "Conjunction extraction:",
-    "- When a sentence lists multiple distinct items with 'and' or commas (e.g. 'OAuth and SSO', 'alerts and digests', 'CSV export and PDF generation'), extract each as a separate entity.",
+    "- When a sentence lists multiple distinct items with 'and' or commas (e.g. 'OAuth and SSO', 'alerts and digests', 'CSV export and PDF generation', 'payment processing and onboarding'), extract EACH item as a separate entity. Do not stop after the first item.",
     "- This applies to both features under a project and projects under a workspace.",
     "",
     "Document text behavior:",
     "- Document/spec text can contain entities without conversational phrasing.",
     "- Extract only the core committed entities; do not split one clause into multiple near-duplicate entities.",
     "- Prefer one dominant entity per clause unless there are clearly separate commitments.",
+    "- A sentence listing what a system will do is NOT multiple tasks. Extract only clauses that represent distinct commitments (decisions, assigned work). Descriptive verbs (ingest, render, process) in a system overview are not separate tasks.",
     "",
     "Examples:",
     "- Input: I decided to use event sourcing for audit logs. => decision text: use event sourcing (no person entity). If 'audit logs' exists in graph context, emit RELATES_TO from decision to that feature.",
@@ -111,7 +112,7 @@ export function buildExtractionSystemPrompt(input: { onboarding: boolean }): str
     "- Include only relationships that are explicitly grounded in the Current source text.",
     "- Each relationship must include kind, fromTempId, toTempId, confidence, fromText, toText.",
     "- Use uppercase snake case relationship kinds when possible (for example DEPENDS_ON, BLOCKS, RELATES_TO).",
-    "- When an extracted entity references an existing graph entity (e.g. a decision mentions an existing feature), emit a RELATES_TO relationship linking them.",
+    "- When an extracted entity references an existing graph entity (e.g. a decision mentions an existing feature by name), ALWAYS emit a RELATES_TO relationship linking the new entity to the existing one. This is critical for graph connectivity.",
     input.onboarding
       ? "Prioritize foundational onboarding entities: projects, first decisions, open questions, and constraints."
       : "Prioritize actionable entities and direct relationships.",
diff --git a/evals/extraction.eval.ts b/evals/extraction.eval.ts
index 84ee99d..bd1f142 100644
--- a/evals/extraction.eval.ts
+++ b/evals/extraction.eval.ts
@@ -410,7 +410,7 @@ function hasEnv(name: string): boolean {
 }
 
 function buildCaseCacheKey(modelId: string, testCase: GoldenCase): string {
-  const cacheVersion = "classification-v11";
+  const cacheVersion = "classification-v12";
   const caseHash = createHash("sha256").update(JSON.stringify(testCase)).digest("hex").slice(0, 24);
   return `${cacheVersion}:${modelId}:${testCase.id}:${caseHash}`;
 }

input.command:
git add app/src/server/extraction/prompt.ts evals/extraction.eval.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Improve extraction prompt for reference resolution, conjunctions, and over-extraction

Strengthen 5 prompt rules to fix failing eval cases:
- Reference resolution: entity text must contain resolved concept name, not referential phrase
- Placeholder rejection: add "the platform", "the system" to vague reference list
- Conjunction extraction: emphasize extracting ALL items, not just the first
- Relationship emission: stronger RELATES_TO rule for graph connectivity
- Document text: descriptive verbs in system overview are not separate tasks

Bump eval cache version to v12 to force re-extraction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
input.description: Commit prompt and eval cache changes

content.thinking:
OK, so the eval actually did run with the new prompt (Duration: 21999ms vs 19601ms before, and results changed). Let me compare the results:

**Improved cases:**
1. **long-context-first-option-resolution**: 72% → 97% (Prec: 0→1.00, Rec: 0→1.00) - FIXED!
2. **document-like-chunk-1**: 94% → 100% (Ent: 5/2→2/2, Prec: 0.40→1.00) - FIXED!


3. **tool-considered-then-chosen**: 95% → 100% - improved
4. **category-task-design**: 100% → 100% - stayed same
5. **category-decision-operations**: 100% → 95% - slight regression but still passing

But I'm seeing some concerning regressions too. **grounded-evidence** dropped from 95% to 72% with both precision and recall going to zero, which is a new problem. And **category-task-operations** fell from 100% to 88%, though the details are cut off.

A few cases are still stuck: **decision-with-feature-link** at 88%, **project-no-keyword-launch** at 92%, and **feature-within-project** at 92%. The net effect is that I fixed two cases but introduced two regressions, so I'm essentially back where I started.

Let me dig into what went wrong with **grounded-evidence** specifically, since that's the most dramatic drop.

content.text:
Good progress! The prompt changes fixed 2 of the original 5 failures but introduced 2 regressions. Let me compare:

**Fixed:**
- `long-context-first-option-resolution`: 72% → 97%
- `document-like-chunk-1`: 94% → 100%

**New regressions:**
- `grounded-evidence`: 95% → 72% (Prec/Rec both 0!)
- `category-task-operations`: 100% → 88% (2/1 entities, over-extracting)

**Still failing:**
- `decision-with-feature-link`: 88%
- `project-no-keyword-launch`: 92%
- `feature-within-project`: 92%

Let me check the cache for the regressed cases to understand what went wrong.

input.command:
python3 -c "
import json
data = json.load(open('eval-results/cache/extraction-cache.json'))
failing = ['grounded-evidence', 'category-task-operations', 'decision-with-feature-link', 'project-no-keyword-launch', 'feature-within-project']
for key, val in data.items():
    case_id = val.get('caseId', '')
    if case_id in failing:
        print(f'=== {case_id} ===')
        print(f'  Cache key version: {key.split(\":\")[0]}')
        print(f'  Input: {val.get(\"input\", \"\")[:150]}')
        print(f'  Extracted entities:')
        for e in val.get('extractedEntities', []):
            print(f'    - kind={e[\"kind\"]}, text={e[\"text\"]}, conf={e.get(\"confidence\",\"?\")}, cat={e.get(\"category\",\"n/a\")}')
        print(f'  Extracted tools: {val.get(\"extractedTools\", [])}')
        rels = val.get('extractedRelations', [])
        if rels:
            print(f'  Relations:')
            for r in rels:
                print(f'    - {r[\"kind\"]}: {r.get(\"fromText\",\"?\")} -> {r.get(\"toText\",\"?\")}')
        print()
"

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have all the information needed to provide a comprehensive answer. Let me create a detailed report.

## Summary: Graph Context Formatting in Extraction Prompt

Based on my analysis of the codebase, here's exactly how graph context is formatted and included in the extraction prompt:

### 1. **Flow Overview**

When the user sends a message, the extraction pipeline works as follows:

```
chat-processor.ts (line 56)
  └─> loadConversationGraphContext() 
       └─> extractStructuredGraph() 
           └─> formatExtractionGraphContext() [formats for prompt]
```

### 2. **Graph Context Data Structure**

The `ExtractionGraphContextRow` type (from `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/palembang/app/src/server/extraction/types.ts` lines 29-35):

```typescript
export type ExtractionGraphContextRow = {
  id: GraphEntityRecord;           // RecordId with table info (project, feature, task, etc)
  kind: ExtractableEntityKind;     // "project" | "feature" | "task" | "decision" | "question"
  text: string;                    // Actual entity name/title/summary
  confidence: number;              // 0-1 confidence score
  sourceMessage: RecordId<"message", string>;  // Which message it came from
};
```

### 3. **How Graph Context is Loaded**

From `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/palembang/app/src/server/extraction/context-loaders.ts` (lines 58-114):

- Queries `extraction_relation` table to find all entities extracted in this conversation
- Ordered by `extracted_at DESC`, limited to 60 most recent items
- Deduplicates by entity ID (only first occurrence kept)
- Only includes valid extractable types: project, person, feature, task, decision, question
- Reads the actual entity text via `readEntityText()`:
  - For **feature**: reads `name` field
  - For **project**: reads `name` field  
  - For **task**: reads `title` field
  - For **decision**: reads `summary` field
  - For **question**: reads `text` field

### 4. **Prompt Formatting**

From `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/palembang/app/src/server/extraction/extract-graph.ts` (lines 96-107):

```typescript
function formatExtractionGraphContext(rows: ExtractionGraphContextRow[]): string {
  if (rows.length === 0) {
    return "(no prior extracted entities)";
  }

  return rows
    .map((row) => {
      const table = row.id.tb;
      return `[entity:${table}:${row.id.id as string}] ${row.kind}: ${row.text} (confidence ${row.confidence.toFixed(2)}, source message ${row.sourceMessage.id as string})`;
    })
    .join("\n");
}
```

**Example output for the prompt:**
```
[entity:feature:abc123] feature: audit logs (confidence 0.95, source message msg-456)
[entity:feature:def456] feature: user authentication (confidence 0.88, source message msg-789)
[entity:project:ghi789] project: Payment System (confidence 0.92, source message msg-123)
```

### 5. **Workspace Scope Context**

Additionally, existing projects are formatted separately (lines 109-122):

```typescript
function formatWorkspaceScope(workspaceName?: string, projectNames?: string[]): string {
  if (!workspaceName) {
    return "(no workspace context)";
  }

  const lines = [`Workspace: "${workspaceName}"`];
  if (projectNames && projectNames.length > 0) {
    lines.push(`Existing projects: ${projectNames.map((name) => `"${name}"`).join(", ")}`);
  } else {
    lines.push("Existing projects: none");
  }

  return lines.join("\n");
}
```

**Example output:**
```
Workspace: "Marcus's Brain"
Existing projects: "AI-Native Business Management Platform", "Payment System"
```

### 6. **Complete Prompt Assembly**

From `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/palembang/app/src/server/extraction/extract-graph.ts` (lines 32-59), the full prompt structure is:

```
[System prompt about extraction rules]

Conversation history (reference resolution only):
[formatted conversation history]

Current message (extract only from this text when message metadata exists):
[current user message]

Resolved-from lineage constraints:
[allowed resolvedFromMessageId values]

Existing graph context (semantic index of prior extracted entities):
[formatExtractionGraphContext output - ALL prior entities with confidence scores]

Workspace scope:
[formatWorkspaceScope output - workspace name + existing project list]

[Optional section heading if present]

Current source text:
[the actual user message text to extract from]
```

### 7. **Yes, Seeded Features Are Included!**

When a feature like "audit logs" is seeded in the workspace:

1. **During onboarding** (in `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/palembang/app/src/server/extraction/context-loaders.ts`):
   - The feature gets extracted and stored in SurrealDB
   - Its record appears in `extraction_relation` with `confidence` and source message

2. **In subsequent messages**:
   - `loadConversationGraphContext()` retrieves the feature from `extraction_relation`
   - Its `name` is fetched from the feature record
   - It appears in the prompt as:
     ```
     [entity:feature:feature-uuid] feature: audit logs (confidence 0.95, source message onboarding-msg-id)
     ```

3. **Model sees it for reference resolution**:
   - The prompt instruction (lines 15-20 in prompt.ts) tells the model:
     > "Conversation history is only for reference resolution... When an extracted entity references an existing graph entity (e.g. a decision mentions an existing feature by name), ALWAYS emit a RELATES_TO relationship linking the new entity to the existing one."

### 8. **Key Difference: Conversation History vs Graph Context**

- **Conversation History** (last 30 messages): Raw text for reference resolution (pronouns, "it", "that")
- **Graph Context** (last 60 extracted entities): Structured entities with confidence scores, explicitly marked with table type and entity ID, for semantic linking

### File Locations Summary

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/palembang/app/src/server/extraction/extract-graph.ts` | Main orchestration + formatting functions |
| `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/palembang/app/src/server/extraction/context-loaders.ts` | Loads graph context from database |
| `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/palembang/app/src/server/extraction/prompt.ts` | System prompt with extraction rules |
| `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/palembang/app/src/server/chat/chat-processor.ts` | Calls extractStructuredGraph with loaded context |
| `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/palembang/app/src/server/extraction/entity-text.ts` | Reads entity name/title/summary by kind |

Good, so the graph context IS included in the prompt and the seeded features should be visible. The issue is that the model sees the graph context but doesn't emit RELATES_TO relationships.

Now I need to fix:
1. **grounded-evidence** (72%): Model extracts `task: "prioritize the onboarding summary card"` but expected `feature: "onboarding summary card"`. The rule about prioritization/scoping → feature exists but isn't being followed. I need to add a more direct example.

2. **category-task-operations** (88%): Model over-extracts, creating both `project: "CI/CD pipeline for staging"` and `task: "set up the CI/CD pipeline for staging"`. Expected just the task. 

The rule about not extracting the subject as a separate project exists but isn't being applied.

3. **decision-with-feature-link** (88%): RELATES_TO relationship not emitted despite rule. The graph context includes "audit logs" as a feature. Maybe I need to make the instruction more specific about HOW to emit the relationship (use the entity ID from graph context).

4. **project-no-keyword-launch** (92%): Conjunction failure - only "payment processing" extracted, missing "onboarding".

5. **feature-within-project** (92%): Conjunction failure - only "OAuth support" extracted, missing "SSO".

Let me approach this systematically by identifying which issues are rule-following problems versus parsing problems.

For #1 (grounded-evidence): I need a direct example showing that "We will prioritize X" means extract the feature X with prioritization as scoping, not as executable work.

For #2 (category-task-operations): The rule is clear but not being followed - when a task describes work on a subject like "research pricing models", I should extract only the task, not the subject as a separate project. Adding a concrete example should help.

For #3 (decision-with-feature-link): The decision gets extracted correctly but the RELATES_TO relationship doesn't get created. The model might not understand what "existing graph entity" means in the prompt format, so I need to be more explicit about using the entity ID from the graph context.

For #4 and #5, the issue is different - both already have `multi_allowed` intent but recall is only 50%, meaning half the expected entities aren't being found. Let me examine why some conjunctions work while these specific cases fail, since the examples are already in the prompt.

Looking at the working cases, they all involve either multiple distinct entity types or explicit inline mentions. But for `feature-within-project`, the model extracts "OAuth support" while missing SSO from "OAuth and SSO" - it's even adding "support" to the first entity but not recognizing the second one in the same pattern.

The `project-no-keyword-launch` case is similar: "payment processing and user onboarding flows" yields only the first item. These are straightforward conjunctions that should work, and I notice the exact example is already in the prompt. Maybe the issue is that slight wording variations between the prompt example and the actual test case are preventing proper generalization. Let me check if the prompt's phrasing matches what I'm testing, or if I need to make the conjunction rule more explicit in the instructions. I need to check which extraction model is being used from the environment variables. Rather than trying to make the test cases more lenient, I should focus on fixing the three cases where I can actually improve performance: the grounded-evidence case needs better distinction between scoping actions like "prioritize" and executable tasks, the category-task-operations case requires stricter rules against extracting subjects as separate projects, and the decision-with-feature-link case is missing relationship connections. Once I address these, I can reassess the overall score.

1→export function buildExtractionSystemPrompt(input: { onboarding: boolean }): string {
     2→  return [
     3→    "Extract structured business entities and relationships from the provided text.",
     4→    "Return only high-confidence extractions with explicit entity references.",
     5→    "Entity kinds: project, feature, task, decision, question.",
     6→    "Never emit kind: person.",
     7→    "If a person is explicitly tied to an entity, set assignee_name on that entity using the literal source name.",

     9→    "Output schema rules:",

    11→    "- Entity text must be specific and concrete, not generic placeholders.",
    12→    "- evidence must be a verbatim snippet from Current source text.",
    13→    "- Always include tools and relationships arrays (can be empty).",
    14→    "- confidence is a number between 0 and 1.",
    15→    "",
    16→    "Source grounding and context rules:",
    17→    "- When Current message metadata exists, extract from Current source text only.",
    18→    "- Conversation history is only for reference resolution (that/it/this/first option).",
    19→    "- History must never add new entities that are absent from Current source text intent.",
    20→    "- Do not copy assistant-only rationale/details from history into entity text.",
    21→    "- Use the shortest canonical concept phrase when resolving references. Entity text must always contain the resolved concept name (e.g. 'SurrealDB'), never the referential phrase (e.g. 'the first option').",
    22→    "- If entity meaning requires context resolution, include resolvedFromMessageId as the raw message id string only (UUID).",
    23→    "- Never include wrappers or prefixes in resolvedFromMessageId (no [message:...], no message:...).",
    24→    "- resolvedFromMessageId must be one of the allowed history message ids listed in the prompt section; otherwise omit it.",
    25→    "- Prefer user messages as resolvedFromMessageId anchors when both user and assistant mention the same concept.",
    26→    "- Never set resolvedFromMessageId to Current message id.",
    27→    "- If Current message metadata is unavailable (document chunk extraction), do not emit resolvedFromMessageId.",
    28→    "- If a confirmation cannot resolve to one specific concept, emit no entity.",
    29→    "",
    30→    "Kind classification rules:",
    31→    "- Project: named product, system, or initiative that is a top-level workstream within the workspace (building, launching, creating, developing; identified by a proper noun or product name).",
    32→    "- Decision: commitment/selection language (let's go with, move forward with, we decided, we'll use, I'm choosing, going with, settled on, committed to, approved).",
    33→    "- Task: concrete executable work with an action verb (implement, build, fix, migrate, deploy, set up, write, test, review, handle, research, investigate, evaluate, analyze, explore).",
    34→    "- Feature: capability/requirement or scoped artifact description (supports, provides, enables, needs to handle, must support, we need, requires; cards/dashboards/flows/screens/integrations).",
    35→    "- Question: explicit question framing or question mark.",
    36→    "- Decision vs feature test: did the user CHOOSE between alternatives, or DESCRIBE something to build? Choice language (X instead of Y, go with X, decided on X over Y) = one decision for the chosen option. Description language (we need X, build X) = feature. Even if the chosen option sounds like a feature, commitment language makes it a decision.",
    37→    "- Prioritization/scoping statements about a specific capability without explicit execution action are feature (not project-level initiatives).",
    38→    "- When the user explicitly labels something with a kind word ('the main feature is X', 'our project is called Y'), respect that classification unless workspace scope rules override it.",
    39→    "- When a task describes work on a subject ('research pricing models'), extract only the task, not the subject as a separate project or feature.",
    40→    "",
    41→    "Category classification rules:",
    42→    "- For task, decision, and question entities, classify category based on the area the entity affects.",
